article.body_preview:
DHAKA (Reuters) - Apparel makers in Bangladesh are considering rare bulk exports by air to Europe and the United States, as they race to meet deadlines and avoid cancellations after a cyclone delayed shipments, caused power cuts and disrupted production.

The crunch could disrupt summer clothes supplies for retailers such as Walmart, Gap Inc, H&M, VF Corp, Zara and American Eagle Outfitters, some of Bangladesh's largest export customers.

Cyclone Mocha, one of the strongest storms to hit the reg

llm_connections.evidence_quote: after a cyclone delayed shipments, caused power cuts and disrupted production.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly describes shipment delays caused by the cyclone, matching the Delivery feature's logistics-performance scope (shipment delays affecting exports).

llm_connections.evidence_quote: after a cyclone delayed shipments, caused power cuts and disrupted production.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article states production was disrupted by the cyclone, which directly pertains to the Production feature's scope of manufactured output.

llm_connections.evidence_quote: Power cuts in the last two months, first due to a scorching heatwave, and then the cyclone, have put more stress on apparel companies.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article links power cuts (an operational disruption) to stress on apparel firms, matching the Factory_Issue definition covering power outages and operational disruptions.

llm_connections.evidence_quote: if goods cannot be delivered on time buyers either seek heavy discounts or threaten to cancel orders.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explains missed deliveries lead buyers to demand discounts or cancel orders, which directly affects order fulfillment relative to sales as defined by delivery_sales_ratio.
